title: Israeli Strikes Kill 12 Across Gaza in Coordinated Attacks on Police Checkpoints
detail: Israeli forces carried out multiple simultaneous strikes across Gaza on April 24–25, 2026, killing at least 12 Palestinians. In Khan Younis, an airstrike on a police vehicle near a wedding hall killed seven people including four police officers and three civilian bystanders. In Gaza City's Sheikh Radwan neighborhood, a strike near a bank killed three including two police officers. In Beit Lahiya, tank shelling killed two more. Gaza's interior ministry condemned the attacks as an 'unprecedented bloody, fascist approach,' calling on international organizations to hold Israel accountable. The IDF said the strikes targeted Hamas militants.

media.caption: Israeli strikes kill at least 12 Palestinians across Gaza including police officers and civilians

title: UN Human Rights Chief: Gaza Remains Unsafe Six Months After Ceasefire Announcement
detail: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk issued a statement on April 25, 2026, declaring that Palestinians across Gaza remain unsafe six months after the ceasefire announcement, condemning Israel's continued daily strikes as ceasefire violations. The IRC separately warned that 'Gaza's deepening humanitarian catastrophe cannot be forgotten as the world's attention shifts away,' noting the parallel Iran conflict is diverting global focus from Gaza. OCHA's April 23 situational report documented 22 Palestinians killed in the week of April 15–21 alone, and noted that while aid volumes improved (17,400 pallets offloaded April 14–20 vs. 7,400 the prior week), supplies remain far below the 600+ trucks/day threshold needed.

media.caption: OHCHR High Commissioner Volker Türk: Gaza remains unsafe six months after ceasefire

title: UN Women: 38,000+ Women and Girls Killed in Gaza Between Oct 2023 and Dec 2025
detail: UN Women released a report on April 24–25, 2026, documenting that at least 38,000 women and girls were killed in Gaza between October 2023 and December 2025 — an average of 47 per day over the 26-month period. The report highlighted the disproportionate impact of the conflict on women and girls, including the mass killing of pregnant women and nursing mothers, systematic destruction of maternal health infrastructure, and the collapse of gender-based violence services. The findings add to the body of evidence cited in ICJ and ICC proceedings against Israeli officials.

media.caption: UN Women report: 38,000+ women and girls killed in Gaza between Oct 2023 and Dec 2025
